title: EU Inc. - When Europe Forces Us to Evolve
summary: The European Commission proposed EU Inc., a pan-European company registration framework allowing fully digital incorporation across all 27 member states within 48 hours for under €100. The author argues this exposes Romania's bureaucratic failures and could render national corporate frameworks irrelevant for high-value digital companies if states don't modernize.
talking_points: EU Inc. creates a 28th optional legal framework for company registration across the EU, bypassing cumbersome national bureaucracies
Romanian entrepreneurs currently face extreme bureaucratic hurdles including lengthy notarization, stamp requirements, and costly fiscal compliance that drives business elsewhere
The reform democratizes access to European markets for founders who previously needed expensive lawyers or had to register in Estonia, Netherlands, or other business-friendly jurisdictions
While EU Inc. doesn't change tax obligations, it creates competitive pressure on member states to offer stable, predictable fiscal environments
European trade unions have criticized the proposal as a 'Trojan horse' threatening workers' rights, but the author argues the solution is adding social guarantees rather than blocking reform
author: Andrei Colțea
